title: Test a CLI end to end
description: Drive the built binary with arguments and stdin, then assert on stdout, stderr, and exit code separately.
body: Steps: 1. Build/locate the actual executable (test the real entry point, not just internal functions). 2. For each case, run it with specific args and piped stdin. 3. Assert separately on stdout (data), stderr (diagnostics), and the exit code. 4. Cover success, each documented failure, --help, unknown flags, and empty/large stdin. 5. Use golden files for stable output, normalizing volatile bits (timestamps, paths). Rollback point: before changing output format (update goldens deliberately). Validation: tests confirm correct stream routing and exit codes across cases; asserting only on combined output misses stream-separation bugs and exit-code regressions.
